data.title: Bitcoin price tags 2-week highs as markets bet big on Trump crypto news
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC) spiked to two-week highs on March 20 amid rumors that the US government was preparing a “major update” to its crypto policy.


BTC/USD 4-hour chart. Source: Cointelegraph/TradingView
Fed’s Powell injects relief into stocks, crypto
Data from Cointelegraph Markets Pro and TradingView showed BTC/USD reaching nearly $87,500 on Bitstamp.
Currently consolidating near $86,000, Bitcoin benefitted from a fairly cool Federal Reserve meeting the day prior in which officials opted to hold interest rates at current levels
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bitcoin-price-2-week-highs-markets-bet-big-trump-crypto-news?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Meta AI Expands Text-Based Chatbot to Europe Amid Regulatory Challenges
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Meta is set to launch its AI chatbot across Europe after nearly a year-long delay due to regulatory issues. Starting this week, the text-based chatbot will be available on WhatsApp, Facebook, Instagram, and Messenger in 41 European countries and 21 overseas regions.

Meta spokesperson Ellie Hitric stated that the company has engaged extensively with European regulators over the past year. Currently, the chatbot will operate in a text-only mode and will not be trained on first-party data from EU users. Meta plans to continue collaborating with regulators to ensure that European users can access its AI innovations and receive appropriate services, similar to those available in other parts of the world.

data.title: BitMart Market Report: Sentiment is neutral in the short term, and wait-and-see sentiment is strong
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to BitMart's market report on March 20, the total market value of the cryptocurrency market is 2.81 trillion US dollars, up 3.51% from the previous day. The net inflow of BTC ETF funds is 22 million US dollars, and the net inflow of ETH ETF is 3 million US dollars. The ETF fund flow remains relatively stable. The fear and greed index is 31, which is in the "fear" range. The comprehensive long-short ratio is 0.97. The market sentiment is neutral in the short term, and the wait-and-see sentiment is strong. Investors need to pay close attention to the subsequent fund flows and sentiment changes.
